svg.text: Comparing performance and compatibility for PostgreSQL workloads
What is an Abstract Syntax Tree?

div.style: font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; color: #475569; line-height: 1.4;

foreignObject.div: An AST represents SQL as a tree structure, enabling programs to analyze, transform, validate, or rewrite queries programmatically.

div.style: font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 8.5px; color: #64748b; line-height: 1.5;

foreignObject.div: PostgreSQL logical replication lets applications subscribe to a filtered stream of database changes; only rows matching a SQL predicate (e.g. WHERE region = 'EU') are forwarded. When a subscriber registers its query, the system parses it into a rule tree. Every WAL event (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) is then evaluated against that tree in real time to decide whether to replicate the row. This pattern powers CDC pipelines, event-driven microservices, and cross-region data synchronisation.

div.style: display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Apache DataFusion project. Most adopted Rust SQL parser (50M+ downloads). Multi-dialect. Fuzz tested.

div.style: display: flex; gap: 10px; font-size: 9px; color: #64748b; margin-bottom: 6px;
display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Uses PostgreSQL's actual parser via C FFI. Guaranteed syntax compatibility. Query fingerprinting. pganalyze.

div.style: display: flex; gap: 10px; font-size: 9px; color: #64748b; margin-bottom: 6px;
display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Zero-copy parser for MySQL/MariaDB. PostgreSQL support is experimental. Only parses ~30% of PG SELECT queries.

div.style: display: flex; gap: 10px; font-size: 9px; color: #a16207; margin-bottom: 6px;

span.style: background: #fef08a; color: #78350f; padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 3px; border: 1px solid #eab308;

div.style: display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Multi-dialect SQL transpiler. Pure Rust, WASM-compatible. Fastest bulk parser (1.3–2.5x). Still very early-stage (Feb 2026): 51–61% FP rate, silent pass-through of 22+ untranslated constructs, semantic bugs. Evaluate carefully.

div.style: display: flex; gap: 10px; font-size: 9px; color: #64748b; margin-bottom: 6px;
display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Custom Pratt parser from the Databend cloud data warehouse. Zero-copy, high-performance. 95% TPC-H recall, 55% common SQL, 13% PG-specific (no DDL support).

div.style: display: flex; gap: 10px; font-size: 9px; color: #64748b; margin-bottom: 6px;

svg.text: Time to parse 500 SQL statements concatenated into a single string (lower is better)

g.text: *sql-parse only parses ~30% of PG SELECT queries.
993 queries from Gretel AI synthetic dataset
984 queries from Gretel AI synthetic dataset
pg_query (summary) ~7x faster than full AST, avoids protobuf deserialization.
934 queries from Gretel AI synthetic dataset
DELETE is simplest. FFI overhead proportionally largest here; pure Rust parsers show biggest advantage.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: 1.3–2.5x faster at 1000 stmts. Still early-stage (Feb 2026): 51–61% FP rate, 79–89% fidelity, silent pass-through of 22+ untranslated PostgreSQL constructs.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Skips protobuf deserialization; extracts tables, functions, filter cols. Best for metadata extraction. Use pg_query.rs (full) for guaranteed syntax compat + fingerprinting.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Best single-stmt latency; 99% recall, 100% fidelity on common SQL. Note: 28–30% FP rate. No C deps, fuzz tested, WASM-ready. Best for general use.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-bottom: 4px;

div.div: Zero-copy Pratt parser, second-slowest overall. Near-zero FP rate (0–8%). 95% TPC-H, 55% common, 13% PG-specific recall. Best for ClickHouse/Databend workloads.

div.style: font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 8.5px; color: #475569; line-height: 1.6; text-align: center;

foreignObject.div: Recall: share of valid PG SQL correctly accepted · FP rate: share of invalid SQL wrongly accepted as valid · Fidelity: pg_query canonical(output) == canonical(input), i.e. semantic equivalence to the original · ground truth: pg_query.rs (libpg_query)

div.div: 21 valid / 1 invalid out of 22 (Q1 uses ANSI INTERVAL)

g.text: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 5975WX (32 cores) · Ubuntu 24.04 · Rust 2021 edition · Criterion.rs v0.8
Parsers: sqlparser-rs · polyglot-sql · pg_query.rs (+ summary) · sql-parse · databend
